decisions.title: Adopt SCIM as standard identity management for enterprise customers
decisions.context: Heavy signal volume around SCIM edge cases (deactivation, email changes, sync health, testing infrastructure). This suggests SCIM is moving from 'new feature' to 'critical infrastructure'. Need to decide whether to fully commit resources to hardening vs. treating as optional enterprise add-on.

arguments.for: Aligns with 'Embed Deeper' pillar—SCIM enables enterprise IT integration. 8+ tickets show customer pain points that affect retention. Strengthens moat against competitors lacking enterprise identity features.
arguments.against: Resource-intensive hardening distracts from core product (Explore V2, Canvas). SCIM serves <10% of customer base. May be premature optimization before proving North Star metric improvement.

decisions.title: Prioritize CSRS (Customer-Scoped Resource Service) migration over feature work
decisions.context: Multiple signals (ENG-9460, ENG-9494) indicate ongoing CSRS migration. This is foundational infra that affects query performance and scalability—directly impacts 'Nail the Core' by improving Explore V2 speed and Canvas responsiveness.
decisions.blockedBy: Need capacity planning: 3 engineers for 6 weeks vs. current Sprint commitments to Python Rules and Sift Edge

arguments.for: Unblocks performance improvements for Explore V2 (core product). DataFusion-based architecture is modern, maintainable foundation. Shadow traffic comparison (ENG-9494) shows we're close to production-ready.
arguments.against: No immediate customer-facing benefit—invisible infra work. Delays Python Rules GA and Sift Edge beta. Could incrementally migrate rather than big-bang approach.

decisions.title: Raise calculated channels nesting limit (ENG-9929) vs. invest in performance optimization
decisions.context: Customer hitting nesting limit suggests complex use cases emerging. Quick fix: raise limit. Strategic fix: optimize evaluation engine so limits become irrelevant. Impacts 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' if blocking advanced workflows.

arguments.for: Raising limit is 1-day fix, unblocks customer immediately. Shows responsiveness. Can optimize later when we have more usage data on nesting patterns.
arguments.against: Band-aid solution—doesn't address root performance issue. May encourage inefficient rule design. Could create tech debt if customers build deeply nested logic that's hard to migrate later.

decisions.title: Consolidate internal documentation systems before Q3
decisions.context: ENG-9923 (runbooks migration) and ENG-9838 (external_sync.md updates) signal fragmented docs. Affects onboarding speed and incident response—both critical for scaling team to support 2026 product roadmap.

arguments.for: Reduces cognitive load for engineers context-switching between products (Explore, Canvas, Rules, Edge). Faster incident resolution improves customer trust. Easier to onboard new hires for H2 hiring.
arguments.against: Low ROI—doesn't directly move North Star metric. Engineers can navigate current system. Migration effort could be spent on customer-facing features.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 adoption: 60% of active users
API integration depth: 8+ endpoints per customer

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of work hours) fragmented focus time. Wednesday entirely booked—no deep work possible. SCIM incident retros consumed 4 hours unplanned time.
alerts: Zero uninterrupted 2+ hour blocks Thursday/Friday—impacts strategic thinking time
Recurring SCIM sync review (1hr weekly) could shift to async Loom updates
